model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The run is capped at 256 optimizer steps, so the baseline is severely undertrained. This candidate uses the fixed-step budget more effectively by doubling batch size to expose the model to substantially more unique examples, increasing the learning rate to keep progress per update high, and turning on cosine decay to stabilize the higher initial rate over the short horizon.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The short-horizon cosine decay hurt, so this candidate removes it and shifts to AdamW, which often gives better early-step behavior on small CNNs than plain Adam when weight decay matters. The change keeps the successful larger batch and higher learning rate from step 0 while testing a cleaner optimizer/regularization pairing.","secondary_modes":["caching","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With fixed 256-step evaluation, the next likely lever is throughput per optimizer update. Increasing batch size toward full-dataset coverage should expose the model to many more unique examples within the same step budget, and scaling the learning rate with it keeps optimization progress from becoming too conservative.","secondary_modes":["topk","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The current recipe is training aggressively with large batches and a high learning rate, which can make the logits overconfident early. Adding modest label smoothing is a very cheap regularization change that often improves CIFAR generalization without slowing the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 10","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 8","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 40","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 32","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The optimization recipe has improved substantially, so the next likely bottleneck is model capacity. This is a modest width increase rather than a large redesign, aiming to capture more signal within the same short training horizon while keeping complexity and runtime risk controlled.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After increasing both data per step and model capacity, the optimizer may now be slightly under-driving progress within only 256 updates. A moderate learning-rate increase is a cheap way to test whether the current recipe can convert the extra capacity into faster loss reduction over this short horizon.","secondary_modes":["indexing","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"With only 256 optimizer updates, AdamW may benefit from a shorter second-moment memory than the default `beta2=0.999`. Lowering `beta2` to `0.99` is a targeted short-horizon optimization that can make adaptation more responsive without changing the broader training recipe that is already working well.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The lower `beta2` regressed, so this restores the better AdamW behavior and then reduces weight decay. With only 256 updates and `label_smoothing=0.1` already active, the current recipe may be over-regularized; a lighter decay should let the model fit more signal without changing the overall optimization setup.","secondary_modes":["indexing","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The lighter weight decay did not help, so this restores the better regularization setting and pushes the main lever that has worked best so far: more aggressive progress per update. Given the large batch, short training horizon, and stable training so far, a moderate bump to `4e-3` is a reasonable next test.","secondary_modes":["caching","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 10","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 40","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The higher learning rate overshot, so this restores the stronger `3e-3` setting and spends the next experiment on capacity. The current recipe is fitting quickly and appears optimization-limited less than model-limited now, so a modest additional width increase is a reasonable next attempt at improving the best loss.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 10","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 40","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The extra width regressed, so this returns to the stronger base architecture and tests a slightly lighter form of the regularization that was helping most. `label_smoothing=0.1` improved over no smoothing, but at the current higher-capacity, high-learning-rate recipe it may be a bit too strong for only 256 updates.","secondary_modes":["layout","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),","function":"","new":"","old":"","op":"insert_after","source":"","text":"\n            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),"}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The best results so far came from stronger optimization rather than heavier width, which suggests the current model may still benefit from a little more depth before it saturates. Adding one more residual block is a minimal architectural change that increases effective capacity and receptive-field mixing without changing the surrounding training recipe.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","old":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The extra depth and lighter smoothing both regressed, so this returns to the strongest regularized architecture and shifts the next experiment to the data-per-step tradeoff. A larger batch is a natural next probe because throughput per update has been one of the most reliable levers in this fixed-step setting.","secondary_modes":["caching","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"            loss.backward()","function":"","new":"","old":"","op":"insert_after","source":"","text":"\n            nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), 1.0)"}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The larger batch regressed, so this returns to the stronger `256`-batch regime and uses the remaining experiment budget on a small stability tweak. With aggressive `AdamW`, `3e-3` learning rate, and only 256 updates, mild gradient clipping is a cheap way to reduce occasional overshoot without changing the overall recipe that produced the best loss so far.","secondary_modes":["topk","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"","old":"","op":"delete_exact","source":"","text":"            nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), 1.0)"},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"            layers1.insert(1, nn.BatchNorm2d(channels, momentum=0.2))\n            layers2.insert(1, nn.BatchNorm2d(channels, momentum=0.2))","old":"            layers1.insert(1, nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))\n            layers2.insert(1, nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"            stem_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(channels, momentum=0.2))","old":"            stem_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels, momentum=0.2) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),","old":"            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Gradient clipping did not help, so this removes it and targets a short-horizon issue that is more specific to this setup: batch-norm running statistics. With only 256 training steps, slightly higher BN momentum can let evaluation statistics adapt faster to the trained weights without otherwise changing the best-performing optimization recipe.","secondary_modes":["layout","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"            layers1.insert(1, nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))\n            layers2.insert(1, nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))","old":"            layers1.insert(1, nn.BatchNorm2d(channels, momentum=0.2))\n            layers2.insert(1, nn.BatchNorm2d(channels, momentum=0.2))","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"            stem_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))","old":"            stem_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(channels, momentum=0.2))","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),","old":"            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels, momentum=0.2) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The higher BN momentum did not help, so this restores the better normalization behavior and probes a likely remaining sweet spot between the best `3e-3` run and the worse `4e-3` run. A moderate intermediate learning rate is a targeted next test with low complexity and a clear rationale from the observed trend.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.12)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The intermediate learning rate regressed, so this returns to the stronger `3e-3` setting and uses the remaining budget on a narrow regularization sweep around the best-known `label_smoothing=0.1`. Since `0.05` was clearly too low and smoothing has been one of the more effective changes overall, a small upward move is a sensible low-complexity probe.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 224","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.12)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The small smoothing increase did not help, so this restores the stronger `0.1` setting and uses one of the last probes on batch-size granularity. `256` was clearly better than `128` and `384` was worse, so an intermediate batch is a reasonable final attempt to balance dataset coverage per step against optimization noise.","secondary_modes":["caching","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 224","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 4e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The intermediate batch size was clearly worse, so this returns to the strongest `256`-batch setting and uses the final probe on a narrow regularization adjustment around the best-known configuration. A small reduction in AdamW weight decay is a reasonable last attempt because `2e-4` was only slightly worse, suggesting the optimum could sit between `2e-4` and `5e-4`.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}
